worth_it_explanation: Advantage+ creative enhancements change the ad a person sees, applying things like image filters, text overlays, music, and aspect-ratio crops on top of the asset that was uploaded. When the enhancements run without a check against brand guidelines, the rendered ad can drift from what the brand approved. When they are switched off across the board, the account gives up variations that can help reach. Both states are settings in Meta Ads Manager, so the check is reading toggles and comparing the delivered creative against the brand, not rebuilding anything.
intro: Advantage+ creative is a set of enhancements in Meta Ads Manager that Meta applies on top of an uploaded image or video, including brightness and contrast adjustments, artistic filters, text and music additions, and aspect-ratio variations. Some enhancements default to on at the ad level, and the delivered ad can differ from the asset the brand signed off on. These checks confirm that enhancements are reviewed against brand guidelines rather than applied unseen, and that enhancements which would help are not switched off without a reason.

audit_checks.title: Advantage+ creative enhancements are applied without a check against brand guidelines
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Enhancements such as filters, text overlays, and music change the rendered ad away from the approved asset. The delivered creative can break brand color, typography, or tone without the advertiser seeing the variation, which puts brand consistency at risk on ads that are live.

educate.base:
Advantage+ creative enhancements are adjustments Meta applies to an uploaded asset at the ad level, including brightness and contrast changes, artistic filters, text overlays drawn from the ad copy, background music, and aspect-ratio crops that refit the asset for different placements. Several of these default to on when an ad is built in Meta Ads Manager, so the rendered ad a person sees can differ from the asset the brand approved.

The risk is brand drift. A filter can shift a brand color, an automatic crop can cut a logo or a key product detail out of frame, and a text overlay can place words over the part of the image the design depended on. Each change happens after sign-off, so the approved asset and the delivered ad are no longer the same thing.

Meta exposes these enhancements per ad, and they can be reviewed and toggled individually or in bulk across ads. Reviewing the delivered variation against the brand guidelines, rather than the uploaded source asset, is the way to see what the audience actually receives.

A few signs point to enhancements running without review:
  • Enhancements are on at the ad level and no one on the brand side has seen the rendered variations
  • The delivered ad shows a filter, crop, or overlay that the source asset did not have
  • A logo or a required legal line sits partly out of frame after an automatic crop
You may want to compare the delivered creative in the ad preview against the brand guidelines before deciding the enhancements are acceptable.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a store, automatic crops and overlays can cut across product imagery, so a price callout, a logo, or a required disclosure can fall partly out of frame in a placement the brand never previewed. You may want to step through the ad preview for each placement and confirm the product and any required text stay fully in frame.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation account, the creative often carries an offer or a regulated claim, and a text overlay or filter can obscure or alter how that claim reads. You may want to confirm the offer text and any compliance wording remain legible and unchanged in the delivered variation across placements.
site_type_overlays.default: You may want to review the delivered ad variation against the brand guidelines for each placement, rather than the uploaded source asset, to confirm filters, crops, and overlays have not changed brand color, typography, or required text.

learn_more.source_title: Create an ad with Advantage+ creative

investigate.intro: Open the ads in Meta Ads Manager, read which Advantage+ creative enhancements are on at the ad level, and compare the delivered variation in the preview against the brand guidelines.

meta_ads_manager.label: Meta Ads Manager
steps: Open Meta Ads Manager and select an active campaign, then open an ad at the ad level.
In the ad's creative settings, find the Advantage+ creative section and note which enhancements are on, for example filters, music, text overlays, or aspect-ratio adjustments.

Open the ad preview and step through the placements. Compare the rendered creative against the brand guidelines, watching for shifted colors, cropped logos, and overlays that cover important parts of the image.
Repeat across a representative set of active ads so the review covers the enhancements actually in delivery, not a single example.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active ad reviewed, which Advantage+ creative enhancements are on and whether the delivered variation in the preview matches the brand guidelines. An ad whose enhancements are on but whose rendered creative holds to brand color, typography, framing, and required text passes. An ad whose delivered variation drifts from the brand, for example a shifted color or a cropped logo, is a fail recorded with the ad name and the enhancement at fault.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the enhancement settings and the brand-match observation for each ad reviewed above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that Advantage+ creative enhancements are active on {{site_name}}'s ads without a review against the brand guidelines. Meta applies filters, crops, overlays, and similar changes on top of the approved asset, so the ad a person sees can differ from what was signed off, which puts brand consistency at risk. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the delivered variation in the ad preview for each placement, comparing it against the brand guidelines, and turning off any enhancement that changes brand color, typography, framing, or required text. Where the enhancements hold to brand, they can stay on.

audit_checks.title: Advantage+ creative enhancements are switched off across the account without a reason
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Turning off all enhancements removes the placement-fit crops and the personalized variations Meta would otherwise serve. The ads still run, but they forgo reach and delivery flexibility the account could have kept, and the loss is invisible since nothing breaks.

educate.base:
Advantage+ creative enhancements can be turned off individually or in bulk in Meta Ads Manager. Some advertisers switch every enhancement off to keep tight control of the creative, which is a valid choice when brand guidelines are strict. The problem is a blanket off across the whole account applied without a reason, often a setting copied from one ad to the next or a default someone disabled long ago and never revisited.

With enhancements off, Meta serves the uploaded asset as-is. The account gives up the aspect-ratio variations that refit the creative for Stories, Reels, and Feed, and it gives up the personalized variations Meta would otherwise test. For placements that need a different shape than the source asset, an ad with no crop variation can be letterboxed or cropped awkwardly by the placement itself.

The distinction worth drawing is between off on purpose and off by inertia. Off on purpose ties to a brand rule, a regulated claim that cannot be altered, or a tested decision. Off by inertia is a toggle no one chose for a reason.

A few signs suggest enhancements are off without a deliberate reason:
  • Every ad in the account has the same enhancements disabled with no brand rule behind it
  • Placements that need a vertical format are running a square or horizontal source asset with no crop variation
  • No one can point to why the enhancements were turned off
You may want to confirm whether a brand or compliance reason backs the off state before recommending any change.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a store, the same product creative often needs to run across Feed, Stories, and Reels, so the aspect-ratio enhancements help the asset fit each placement. You may want to confirm that disabling enhancements is a deliberate brand choice rather than a setting that leaves product ads poorly cropped in vertical placements.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation account, a regulated claim can be a real reason to keep enhancements off so the creative cannot be altered. You may want to confirm whether a compliance rule backs the off state, and where none applies, consider re-enabling the placement-fit enhancements.
site_type_overlays.default: You may want to confirm whether a brand or compliance reason backs the disabled state. Where none applies, consider re-enabling the enhancements that improve placement fit and personalization.

learn_more.source_title: Turn off Advantage+ creative enhancements in Meta Ads Manager
Bulk edit Advantage+ creative enhancements in Meta Ads Manager

investigate.intro: Open the ads in Meta Ads Manager, read the Advantage+ creative enhancement settings, and check whether a blanket off state is tied to a brand or compliance reason.

meta_ads_manager.label: Meta Ads Manager
steps: Open Meta Ads Manager and review the Advantage+ creative settings on a representative set of active ads at the ad level.
Note whether enhancements are off across all of them, and which specific enhancements are disabled, for example aspect-ratio adjustments or personalized variations.
For ads running in vertical placements such as Stories or Reels, open the preview and check whether the source asset fits the placement or is letterboxed or awkwardly cropped.
Ask the account owner whether a brand guideline or a compliance rule backs the disabled state. Record the answer alongside the settings.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per ad reviewed, which enhancements are off and whether a brand or compliance reason backs the off state. An ad with enhancements off for a stated brand or compliance reason passes. An ad with enhancements off and no reason behind it, especially where a placement is running an ill-fitting source asset, is a fail recorded with the ad name.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the enhancement settings and whether a reason backs the off state for each ad reviewed above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that Advantage+ creative enhancements are switched off across {{site_name}}'s account with no brand or compliance reason behind the off state. With enhancements off, Meta serves the uploaded asset as-is and the account forgoes the placement-fit crops and personalized variations it could otherwise run, so ads in vertical placements can render poorly cropped. {{voice_recommend}} confirming whether a brand rule backs the off state, and where none applies, re-enabling the enhancements that improve placement fit while reviewing the delivered variations against the brand guidelines.

audit_checks.title: Advantage+ text and music overlays are not checked against brand voice and licensing
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Auto-added text and music are generated by Meta rather than written or licensed by the brand. The added text can misstate an offer or read off-tone, and the music can clash with brand voice, all on ads that are already delivering to an audience.

educate.base:
Two Advantage+ creative enhancements add elements the brand did not supply. The text enhancement draws phrases from the ad's primary text or headline and places them as an overlay on the image or video. The music enhancement adds a track Meta selects from a provided library. Both are generated by Meta at delivery, so the wording and the track are chosen by the system rather than written or picked by the brand.

The added text can restate an offer in words the brand did not approve, which is a problem when the offer is precise or regulated. It can also read off-tone for a brand that controls its voice closely. The added music sets a mood that may not match the brand, and while Meta supplies the track from its own library, a brand with its own sonic identity may not want a system-chosen song on its ads at all.

These two enhancements are listed separately in the Advantage+ creative settings, so each can be reviewed and toggled on its own without turning off the rest. Reviewing the actual overlay text and the selected track in the ad preview, rather than assuming the defaults are fine, is the way to catch a mismatch.

A few signs point to text or music overlays going unchecked:
  • The text overlay restates the offer in wording the brand did not write
  • A music track plays on an ad for a brand that controls its sonic identity
  • No one on the brand side has previewed the overlay text or the track
You may want to review the rendered overlay text and the music track in the preview before deciding these enhancements fit the brand.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a store, an auto-generated text overlay can restate a price or a promotion in wording that does not match the actual offer, for example implying a discount that is not running. You may want to confirm the overlay text matches the current offer exactly since a misstated price on a live ad can mislead shoppers.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation account, a regulated claim or a disclosure has to read precisely, and a generated text overlay can paraphrase it in wording compliance never approved. You may want to confirm any overlay text holds to the approved claim language, and where it cannot, turn the text enhancement off for those ads.
site_type_overlays.default: You may want to read the rendered overlay text and listen to the selected track in the ad preview, confirming both match the brand voice and any offer or claim language before leaving these enhancements on.

investigate.intro: Open the ads in Meta Ads Manager, find the text and music enhancements in the Advantage+ creative settings, and review the rendered overlay text and selected track against brand voice and any offer language.

meta_ads_manager.label: Meta Ads Manager
steps: Open Meta Ads Manager, select an active ad, and open its Advantage+ creative settings at the ad level.
Note whether the text overlay enhancement and the music enhancement are on.

Open the ad preview and read the rendered text overlay word for word, comparing it against the approved offer or claim language. Listen to the selected music track and judge it against the brand voice.
Repeat across a representative set of ads so the review reflects the overlays and tracks actually in delivery.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per ad reviewed, whether the text and music enhancements are on and whether the rendered overlay text and selected track match the brand voice and any offer or claim language. An ad whose overlay text and track hold to the brand and the approved language passes. An ad whose overlay misstates an offer, reads off-tone, or carries an unwanted track is a fail recorded with the ad name and the enhancement at fault.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the text and music enhancement settings and the brand-match observation for each ad reviewed above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that Advantage+ text and music overlays on {{site_name}}'s ads are running without a check against brand voice and offer language. Meta generates the overlay text from the ad copy and selects the music track itself, so the wording and the track reach the audience without brand sign-off, which can misstate an offer or clash with brand voice. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the rendered overlay text and the selected track in the ad preview, confirming the text matches the approved offer or claim language, and turning off the text or music enhancement for any ad where it does not fit.
